description: Payloads targeting nonce-based CSP — leak or reuse nonces via CSS injection, DOM clobbering, or script gadgets

payloads.description: CSS selector targeting nonce attribute — makes nonce visible in DOM
payloads.csp_bypass: CSS attribute selector can detect nonce presence for further attacks

payloads.description: CSS exfiltration of nonce — character-by-character via attribute selectors
payloads.csp_bypass: CSS attribute prefix selector leaks nonce value character by character

payloads.description: Nonce read via DOM — requires unsafe-inline to execute
payloads.csp_bypass: If unsafe-inline exists alongside nonce, nonce can be read from DOM

payloads.description: DOM clobbering — creates document.csp.nonce to confuse nonce validation
payloads.csp_bypass: DOM clobbering may confuse server-side nonce injection logic

payloads.description: Meta CSP override attempt — inject weaker CSP via meta tag
payloads.csp_bypass: Meta CSP may override or weaken HTTP header CSP in some browsers

payloads.description: Dangling comment + nonce — consume nonce from next script tag
payloads.csp_bypass: HTML comment injection may capture nonce from subsequent script element

payloads.description: Empty nonce test — some implementations accept empty nonce
payloads.csp_bypass: Empty nonce attribute may pass validation in buggy implementations

payloads.description: External CSS for nonce exfil — loads CSS with attribute selectors
payloads.csp_bypass: External stylesheet with nonce-targeting attribute selectors
